meta.description:
provides `mount.fuse3.sane`, which behaves identically to `mount.fuse3` except
it supports an additional mount flag, `-o pass_fuse_fd`.

when mounting with `-o pass_fuse_fd`, `mount.fuse3.sane` opens the `/dev/fuse` device (which requires CAP_SYS_ADMIN),
and then `exec`s the userspace implementation, which inherits this file descriptor.
`mount.fuse3.sane` invokes the userspace implementation with the device argument set to something like `/dev/fd/3`, indicating which fd holds the fuse device.

the aim of this flag is to provide a clear handoff point at which the filesystem may drop CAP_SYS_ADMIN.
in this regard, it's much like `-o drop_privileges`, only it leaves the responsibility for that to the fs impl,
in case the fs needs to preserve _other_ privileges besides CAP_SYS_ADMIN.

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only
GNU Lesser General Public License v2.1 only

meta.longDescription:
FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) is an interface for userspace programs to
export a filesystem to the Linux kernel. The FUSE project consists of two
components: The fuse kernel module (maintained in the regular kernel
repositories) and the libfuse userspace library (this package). libfuse
provides the reference implementation for communicating with the FUSE
kernel module.
meta.name: fuse-3.17.4

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.

snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.description: APFS module for linux

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

but without any MAC address checks.

supposed to exit from.

meta.description: APFS module for linux

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.

snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.description: APFS module for linux

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

meta.description: APFS module for linux

license.fullName: GNU General Public License v2.0 only

meta.longDescription:
The Apple File System (APFS) is the copy-on-write filesystem currently
used on all Apple devices. This module provides a degree of experimental
support on Linux.
If you make use of the write support, expect data corruption.
Read-only support is somewhat more complete, with sealed volumes,
snapshots, and all the missing compression algorithms recently added.
Encryption is still not in the works though.

but without any MAC address checks.

supposed to exit from.
